summary: Analyze each Insert-tab Word feature: real behavior + concrete implementation plan for the contenteditable clone

specs.feature: Cover Page (Pages group dropdown): a gallery of built-in cover-page designs (Banded, Facet, Filigree, Ion, etc.). Selecting one inserts a formatted first page with title/subtitle/author/date placeholder fields. Includes "Remove Current Cover Page" action.
specs.wordBehavior: In MS Word, Cover Page is a split/dropdown control under Insert > Pages. Selecting a design from the built-in gallery inserts a single full-page formatted design at the very beginning of the document, pushing all existing content to page 2. Each template features placeholder fields (Title, Subtitle, Author, Date) that are selectable and editable. Selecting "Remove Current Cover Page" deletes the cover page but preserves the rest of the document. Placeholder text shows "[Click to add text]" style hints. Designs vary in background color, gradient, image patterns (geometric, subtle), and text layout (centered, flush-left with accent bar, etc.).
specs.uiNeeded: Two UI surfaces: (1) Dropdown menu in ribbon showing list of ~6 template names + "Remove Current Cover Page"; (2) Cover page templates are rendered as styled HTML inserted into the document, not separate dialogs. Flyout menu is used (consistent with existing Symbol, Equation patterns). No separate preview—templates are named descriptively (Banded, Facet, Filigree, Ion, Motion, Retrospect). Optional: small icon/visual thumbnail in flyout would enhance UX but not required for MVP.
specs.testPlan:
1. **Insert each template:** Click Insert > Cover Page > [each design name]. Verify:
   - Cover page appears at document top (page 1).
   - Existing content pushed to page 2+.
   - No visual corruption (styling, fonts render correctly).
   - Manual page break present between cover and body.

2. **Edit placeholders:** Click on placeholder text (Title, Subtitle, Author, Date). Verify:
   - Text is selectable and editable.
   - Placeholder hint "[Click to add text]" disappears on focus.
   - Typing replaces placeholder text.

3. **Remove cover page:** Insert a cover > Edit some text > Click Insert > Cover Page > Remove Current Cover Page. Verify:
   - Cover page and break removed.
   - Existing content (body text) remains intact on page 1.
   - Document structure is clean (no orphaned divs/paragraphs).

4. **Multiple inserts:** Insert cover > Add body content > Insert another cover. Verify:
   - New cover pushes old cover + content down.
   - Only first cover is removed if "Remove" is used.

5. **PDF export:** Insert cover with custom text > Export to PDF. Verify:
   - Cover page is page 1 in PDF.
   - Styling (colors, bars, gradients) renders correctly in PDF.

6. **Undo/Redo:** Insert cover > Undo. Verify document returns to pre-cover state. Redo. Verify cover reappears.

7. **Accessibility:** Tab through placeholders. Verify all text is keyboard-accessible and screen-reader compatible.

8. **Performance:** Insert cover with large document (100+ pages). Verify no lag, repagination is fast.
subOptions: Add visual thumbnail previews in flyout (minor polish)
Add 'Save Selection to Cover Page Gallery' (stubs to not implemented)
Add online cover page gallery fetch (stubs to not implemented)
Template color customization dialog (future)
Support multiple cover pages with indexed removal (future)

specs.feature: Table tools — Insert Table dropdown enhancements (Insert Table dialog, Draw Table, Convert Text to Table, Excel Spreadsheet, Quick Tables) + contextual table editing (insert/delete rows/columns, merge/split cells, delete table, right-click context menu)
specs.wordBehavior:
PART A: INSERT TABLE DROPDOWN (ribbon Insert > Table)
The existing grid insert works; the dropdown menu items trigger these handlers:

1. "Insert Table..." → Opens a modal dialog with Rows/Columns spinners (like current grid) + options for Auto-fit (off/contents/window), Remember Dimensions checkbox, Allow Spacing Between Cells checkbox. On OK, builds <table> with <tbody>, <tr>, <td>.

2. "Draw Table" → Enter draw mode: cursor becomes crosshair, user clicks-and-drags to define table boundary (stored as a rectangle), then clicks cells to draw internal grid lines. On Escape or double-click, finalize and insert. Honest placeholder: renders a dialog saying "Draw Table mode: click-drag to define table outline, then click to split cells (not interactive in this release). Use Insert Table instead." Insert an empty table on OK.

3. "Convert Text to Table..." → Opens dialog to select delimiter (Tab, Comma, Paragraph, or Custom char). Must have selection; splits each line by delimiter into a row, each segment becomes a <td>. If no selection, show toast "Select text to convert."

4. "Excel Spreadsheet" → Honest placeholder: "Embedding live Excel would require OLE/ActiveX runtime. This clone can reference external files but cannot embed interactive spreadsheets. Use Insert > Pictures to paste a screenshot instead." Toast the message.

5. "Quick Tables" → Flyout of preset table designs (3×3 sample table, 4-column header+3-row data, etc.). Clicking a preset inserts that canned HTML table.

PART B: CONTEXTUAL TABLE EDITING (when caret is inside <table>)
When selection/caret is inside a <table>, the following UI becomes active:

1. RIGHT-CLICK CONTEXT MENU: When right-click lands inside a table cell, a context menu appears with these items:
   - Insert → Rows Above, Rows Below, Columns Left, Columns Right
   - Delete → Delete Row, Delete Column, Delete Table
   - Merge Cells (active only if 2+ cells selected horizontally/vertically)
   - Split Cells → Dialog: Rows/Columns spinners to split the cell grid
   - Table Properties → Honest placeholder: not implemented
   - Borders and Shading → Honest placeholder: not implemented

2. TABLE DESIGN RIBBON (dynamically shown when caret is in table): 
   Because the ribbon is static in this codebase, the Table editing commands (Insert Row, Delete Row, Insert Column, Delete Column, Merge Cells, Split Cells, Delete Table) are ONLY accessible:
   - Via right-click context menu inside a table
   - OR via WC.Commands.run({cmd: 'tableInsertRowAbove'}, etc.) if ribbon-driven
   - For now, implement context menu. If a "Table Design" ribbon tab is added later, these commands are ready.

3. COMMAND DISPATCH (new handlers in commands.js):
   - H.tableInsertRowAbove() → find current <tr>, insertBefore a new <tr> with matching <td> count
   - H.tableInsertRowBelow() → insertAfter
   - H.tableDeleteRow() → remove current <tr> if not the only row
   - H.tableInsertColumnLeft() → iterate all <tr>, insert <td> at column index
   - H.tableInsertColumnRight() → insertAfter
   - H.tableDeleteColumn() → iterate all <tr>, remove <td> at column index
   - H.tableMergeCells() → if 2+ cells selected in contiguous block, merge their content (colspan/rowspan: honest placeholder: leave as separate cells visually, but collapse DOM; true rowspan/colspan layout is complex and deferred)
   - H.tableSplitCell() → dialog to split a cell into rows/columns (splits the cell's content or creates a nested table; honest: just add empty <div>s inside the cell)
   - H.tableDelete() → remove the entire <table>

PART C: TABLE STATE DETECTION (in editor.js)
Add helpers to identify if caret/selection is inside a table:
- currentTable() → traverse up from sel.anchorNode to find <table> or null
- currentTableCell() → find <td> or <th>
- selectedTableCells() → if selection spans multiple cells, collect all <td>/<th> in range
- tableRowCount(), tableColCount() → iterate <tr> / <td> per row

PART D: IMPLEMENTATION LOCATION
- New file: /renderer/js/table-commands.js (140 lines): currentTable, currentTableCell, selectedTableCells, helper fns for insert/delete/merge
- Extend commands.js (H.table* handlers + tableContextMenu dispatcher)
- Extend editor.js (currentTable, currentTableCell, selectedTableCells methods)
- Extend dialogs.js (D.tableInsertDialog, D.convertTextToTable, D.splitCell, D.tableProperties placeholders)
- Extend util.js (WC.tableContextMenu flyout builder)
- CSS: Add table cell focus/selection styling to editor.css (border highlight on selected cells)
- No preload.js changes needed; no main.js changes needed.

PART E: HONEST LIMITATIONS
1. Rowspan/Colspan: Complex to layout. Placeholder: show toast "Rowspan/Colspan merge not implemented; cells appear separate but are in one grid. For advanced layouts, edit the HTML or use a third-party table editor." Merge/Split only works on contiguous 1D blocks (same row or column).
2. Draw Table: Not interactive; offer simplified grid-insert alternative.
3. Excel Spreadsheet: Advise user to export Excel as image and insert as picture.
4. Complex table properties (shading, borders by cell, etc.): Deferred; basic styling via Borders menu works.
5. Nested tables: Will work in the DOM but may cause pagination confusion. Mark as "use caution."

specs.implementationPlan:
IMPLEMENTATION ORDER (buildable top-down):

PHASE 1: TABLE CONTEXT DETECTION (editor.js, table-commands.js) [30 min]
1. Add to editor.js:
   - Editor.currentTable() → scans up from caret, returns <table> or null
   - Editor.currentTableCell() → returns <td>/<th> or null
   - Editor.selectedTableCells() → if selection spans cells, returns array of all cells in range
   - Editor.tableRowIndex(cell) → index of cell's row
   - Editor.tableColIndex(cell) → index of cell's column within that row

2. Create table-commands.js (utilities):
   - findTableParent(node) → up-traverse to <table>
   - findCellParent(node) → up-traverse to <td>/<th>
   - getCellRowIndex(cell)
   - getCellColIndex(cell)
   - getRowCells(row) → array of all <td>/<th> in that <tr>
   - getTableRowCount(table)
   - getTableColCount(table)

PHASE 2: BASIC TABLE EDITING COMMANDS (commands.js + table-commands.js) [45 min]
1. Implement in commands.js:
   H.tableInsertRowAbove = () => tableInsertRow('above')
   H.tableInsertRowBelow = () => tableInsertRow('below')
   H.tableDeleteRow = () => tableDeleteRow()
   H.tableInsertColumnLeft = () => tableInsertColumn('left')
   H.tableInsertColumnRight = () => tableInsertColumn('right')
   H.tableDeleteColumn = () => tableDeleteColumn()
   H.tableDeleteTable = () => tableDelete()
   H.tableMergeCells = () → toast("Cell merge not fully implemented; paste cells together manually")
   H.tableSplitCell = () → WC.Dialogs.splitCellDialog()

2. Implement helpers in table-commands.js:
   - tableInsertRow(dir) → get current <tr>, create new <tr> with <td>s matching colcount, insert before/after
   - tableDeleteRow() → if >1 row, remove current <tr>; if only row, alert "Cannot delete last row; delete entire table instead"
   - tableInsertColumn(dir) → iterate table rows, get col index, insertBefore/insertAfter <td> in each row
   - tableDeleteColumn() → iterate rows, remove <td> at col index; if only col, treat as table delete
   - tableDelete() → find <table>, remove it, set E().dirty=true, repaginate
   - tableMergeCells() → honest placeholder: toast + no-op for now

PHASE 3: RIGHT-CLICK CONTEXT MENU (util.js + commands.js) [40 min]
1. Extend util.js:
   - WC.tableContextMenu(cell) → returns a flyout builder function
   - Builds menu with Insert/Delete/Merge/Split items
   - Each item calls WC.Commands.run({cmd: 'tableInsertRowAbove'}) etc.

2. Add to editor.js init (mousedown handler):
   document.addEventListener('contextmenu', (e) => {
     if (E().node.contains(e.target)) {
       const cell = findCellParent(e.target);
       if (cell) {
         e.preventDefault();
         WC.tableContextMenu(cell, e.clientX, e.clientY);
       }
     }
   });

3. Modify WC.flyout signature to accept x, y for context menu positioning (or create tableContextMenu as separate positioned popup)

PHASE 4: DIALOG ENHANCEMENTS (dialogs.js) [50 min]
1. Replace/enhance D.insertTable:
   - Add spinners for Rows, Columns (exists; keep)
   - Add Auto-fit dropdown: Off (default), Contents, Window
   - Add "Allow spacing between cells" checkbox + pt spinner
   - Add "Remember dimensions" checkbox (save to localStorage)
   - On OK, build table with proper <tbody> structure and optional spacing CSS

2. Add D.convertTextToTable:
   - Text input for delimiter (default \t for Tab)
   - Radio group: Tab, Comma, Paragraph, Other
   - On OK: get selection, split by delimiter, build <table>
   - If no selection: toast error

3. Add D.splitCellDialog:
   - Rows/Cols spinners
   - On OK: call tableInsertCellContent (placeholder: add empty <div>s or small nested table)

4. Add D.tablePropertiesDialog (placeholder):
   - Toast: "Table properties (shading, borders per-cell, etc.) are not implemented. Use Format > Borders for basic borders."

PHASE 5: CSS STYLING (editor.css) [20 min]
1. Add table styles:
   #editor table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin: 8px 0; }
   #editor table td, #editor table th { border: 1px solid #999; padding: 4px 6px; min-width: 40px; min-height: 20px; }
   #editor table td:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--word-blue); outline-offset: -1px; }
   .table-cell-selected { background: rgba(68, 114, 196, 0.2); }

2. Optional: Add .table-with-spacing (if spacing option used) → td { padding: 8px; }

PHASE 6: RIBBON WIRING (commands.js + ribbon-data.js) [30 min]
1. Update ribbon-data.js Table dropdown items to call actual handlers:
   - "Insert Table..." → cmd: 'tableInsertDialog' or handle inline
   - "Draw Table" → cmd: 'tableDrawPlaceholder' (toast placeholder)
   - "Convert Text to Table..." → cmd: 'tableConvertText'
   - "Excel Spreadsheet" → cmd: 'tableExcelPlaceholder' (toast)
   - "Quick Tables" → cmd: 'tableQuickTables'

2. Add handlers in commands.js:
   H.tableInsertDialog = () → D.insertTable() (enhance existing)
   H.tableConvertText = () → if selection, D.convertTextToTable(), else toast
   H.tableDrawPlaceholder = () → WC.toast("Draw Table is a visual tool not implemented in this release. Use Insert Table instead.", "See docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md")
   H.tableExcelPlaceholder = () → WC.notImplemented("Excel Spreadsheet embedding")
   H.tableQuickTables = (c, node) → WC.flyout with preset table grid

PHASE 7: TESTING & DOCS [remaining time]
1. Test scenarios:
   - Insert table via grid, dialog, and Quick Tables
   - Edit table: insert/delete rows and columns at all positions
   - Delete entire table
   - Right-click in table cell → context menu appears
   - Merge/split cells → honest toast if not implemented
   - Convert text (tab-delimited) to table
   - Export doc with tables to PDF

2. Update docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md:
   - Rowspan/Colspan merge: deferred
   - Draw Table interactivity: simplified
   - Excel embedding: not feasible (recommend screenshot)
   - Complex table properties: deferred

ESTIMATED TOTAL TIME: 215 minutes (~3.5 hours) for full spec implementation

specs.feature: Shapes (Illustrations dropdown) - Insert shape gallery with lines, rectangles, basic shapes, block arrows, stars/banners, callouts
specs.wordBehavior: MS Word's Shapes feature inserts drawing primitives as selectable, resizable objects. Users select a shape category (Lines, Rectangles, etc.), pick a shape, then click/drag on the page to place and size it. Shapes are SVG/drawing objects with fill, outline, and shadow properties. Once inserted, shapes remain selectable, can be moved, resized via grab handles, have a context menu for formatting, and support wrapping modes. In this clone, we replicate the core: shape gallery flyout, shape insertion as inline SVG with default size, basic click-to-insert, and resize via CSS. We do NOT implement full drawing canvas, advanced shape effects, or OLE objects.
specs.uiNeeded: 1. A cascading flyout menu from Insert > Shapes dropdown showing shape categories (Recently Used, Lines, Rectangles, Basic Shapes, Block Arrows, Stars and Banners, Callouts). 2. Each category opens a submenu showing 6-12 shape options as visual thumbnails (small SVG previews, 40x40px, in a grid). 3. Clicking a shape inserts it at the cursor position as an inline SVG element (default ~150x100px or ~80px for lines). 4. The inserted shape has a light gray border + corner resize handles (CSS pseudo-elements or small divs) to signal it is selectable. 5. Resize by dragging handles; double-click to edit fill color via a simple picker (flyout with color palette). 6. Basic styling: shapes get a default fill (#4472C4 blue, matching Word), black 1.5px stroke, and optional drop shadow. Lines have no fill, just stroke.
specs.testPlan: 1. Unit Tests (shapes.js or commands.js): Verify ShapeFactory generators produce valid SVG (check for correct viewBox, element counts, attributes). 2. Integration Tests: Insert each shape type (line, rect, rounded rect, ellipse, triangle, etc.) and verify it appears in the editor. 3. Resize Test: Click, drag the handle, verify width/height update in real-time. Test Shift+Drag for aspect ratio lock. 4. Color Test: Double-click a shape, pick a color, verify all fill-able elements update. 5. Delete Test: Select shape, press Delete, verify removal. 6. Undo/Redo: Insert shape, undo (should remove), redo (should restore). Verify insertHTML + repaginate work correctly. 7. Copy/Paste: Insert shape, Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V, verify shape duplicates with intact HTML structure. 8. Persistence: Save document, reopen, verify shape is still there with original fill color and size. 9. Edge Cases: Insert multiple shapes, overlapping shapes, shapes near page breaks, shapes in lists/tables (partial support, may be quirky). 10. UI/UX: Verify flyout positioning, thumbnail legibility, cursor feedback (move/resize handles), touch support (optional, may require pointer events).

specs.feature: Icons + SmartArt + Chart (Illustrations) - Insert Tab Feature Group
specs.wordBehavior:
(a) **Icons**: User clicks Insert > Icons, opens a searchable gallery grid of ~200 monoline SVG icons (existing WC.icon set). User types to filter by name (e.g., "trash", "check", "home"), selects an icon from the filtered results. Icon is inserted inline as a 24x24 SVG element at cursor position. Icon is selectable, can be copied/deleted, and inherits text color via currentColor.

(b) **SmartArt**: User clicks Insert > SmartArt, selects a layout from a gallery (Basic List, Basic Process, Cycle, Hierarchy). Each layout opens a dialog with a structured diagram rendered as HTML/SVG. User edits text in a small input grid (e.g., 3 items for Basic List, 5 nodes for Hierarchy). User clicks "Insert", diagram is inserted as a self-contained div with styled boxes and SVG connectors. Diagram is not fully editable post-insert (user can click text and edit, but cannot change layout).

(c) **Chart**: User clicks Insert > Chart, selects chart type from a popup (Column, Bar, Line, Pie). A small modal dialog opens with a data entry grid (3 series, 5 categories by default, editable). User fills in sample or real numeric data. On "Insert", an inline SVG chart is rendered at cursor position. Chart uses pure JS D3-like axis/scale calculation; no external library. Pie chart uses SVG path arcs; Column/Bar/Line use rect/line elements. Chart is static post-insert (user can delete but not edit data without re-inserting).

### (A) Icons Picker

**commands.js - H.icons handler:**
```
H.icons = () => WC.Dialogs.iconsGallery();
```

**dialogs.js - D.iconsGallery:**
- Build a modal with a text input for filtering
- On input change, filter the icon set (iterate WC.icons.ICON_NAMES)
- Render a grid of icon cells in a flyout/modal
- Each cell: 32x32 box containing the 24x24 SVG icon (via WC.icon) + text label below
- On click, call E().insertHTML with `<svg ... class="inline-icon" ...>${WC.rawIcon(iconName, 24)}</svg>`
- CSS: `.inline-icon { display: inline; width: 1em; height: 1em; vertical-align: -0.125em; margin: 0 2px; color: currentColor; }`

**icons.js - expose icon metadata:**
Add at the end:
```javascript
window.WC.icons = {
  ICON_NAMES: Object.keys(P).filter(k => k !== '_generic'),
  get(name) { return P[name] || P._generic; },
};
```

**Feasibility**: FULL — the icon SVGs are all defined inline; filtering is pure string matching.

### (B) SmartArt

**smartart.js (new file):**
Define four layout templates:
```javascript
const LAYOUTS = {
  basicList: {
    name: 'Basic List',
    maxItems: 5,
    render(items) {
      // return HTML: div with 5 stacked boxes, each with a text item
      // style: box-shadow, padding, margin-bottom
      // boxes are connected by a vertical line on the left
    }
  },
  basicProcess: {
    name: 'Basic Process',
    maxItems: 5,
    render(items) {
      // return HTML: div with 5 boxes arranged horizontally
      // connected by SVG arrows pointing right
      // last box has no arrow
    }
  },
  cycle: {
    name: 'Cycle',
    maxItems: 4,
    render(items) {
      // return HTML: 4 boxes arranged in a circle
      // connected by curved SVG arrows pointing clockwise
    }
  },
  hierarchy: {
    name: 'Hierarchy',
    maxItems: 7,
    render(items) {
      // return HTML/SVG:
      // 1 box at top (CEO)
      // 3 boxes below (reports)
      // 3 boxes at bottom (sub-reports)
      // connected by SVG lines
    }
  }
};
```

Each layout's render() returns a complete HTML string with inlined <style> + SVG connectors.

**dialogs.js - D.smartArtGallery & D.smartArtDataEditor:**
- Gallery: display 4 thumbnail previews (generated by each layout.render(['Item 1', 'Item 2', ...])), clickable
- Data Editor: on layout selection, open a modal with a grid of text inputs (one per item), live preview pane, Insert/Cancel buttons
- On Insert, call E().insertHTML with the result of layout.render(itemArray), wrapped in a <div class="smartart" data-layout="..."> for future reference

**Feasibility**: FULL — all rendering is pure HTML + SVG path data. No layout/measurement engine needed; all dimensions are fixed (e.g., Basic List boxes are 200x60px each, spaced 10px apart).

### (C) Chart

**chart.js (new file):**
Chart type definitions and SVG render functions:
```javascript
const CHARTS = {
  column: { name: 'Column', render(data, categories) { /* render vertical bars */ } },
  bar: { name: 'Bar', render(data, categories) { /* render horizontal bars */ } },
  line: { name: 'Line', render(data, categories) { /* render line path */ } },
  pie: { name: 'Pie', render(data) { /* render pie arcs */ } },
};
```

Each render(data, categories) takes:
- data: Array<{name: string, values: number[]}> (e.g., [{ name: 'Series A', values: [10, 20, 15] }])
- categories: Array<string> (e.g., ['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3'])
- Returns: `<svg viewBox="0 0 500 300">...</svg>` string

**Scale calculation (no external library):**
```javascript
function linearScale(min, max, count) {
  const step = (max - min) / (count - 1);
  return Array(count).fill(0).map((_, i) => min + step * i);
}

function normalize(value, min, max) {
  return (value - min) / (max - min);
}
```

**Column chart example:**
- SVG width 500, height 300
- Y-axis from 0 to max(data) * 1.1 (with grid lines)
- X-axis with category labels
- For each series, draw vertical bars (width proportional to series count, offset for multi-series)
- Bars use rect elements with a data-driven fill color (cycle through colors from WC.colorPalette colors)

**Line chart example:**
- Same axes as column
- For each series, calculate (x, y) points and draw an SVG <path> with stroke
- Optionally add circle markers at each point

**Pie chart example:**
- SVG 300x300 with a circle in the center
- Calculate slice angles for each data point: angle = (value / sum(values)) * 360
- Draw SVG path arcs using the arc command (A rx ry x-axis-rotation large-arc-flag sweep-flag x y)
- Use distinct colors for each slice
- Legend on the right showing slice labels + values

**dialogs.js - D.chartDialog:**
- Modal title: "Insert Chart"
- Radio buttons or tabs: Column | Bar | Line | Pie
- Data entry grid: 3 rows × 6 columns (3 series × 5 data points + 1 category-label column)
- Default data: Series A/B/C with sample values (10, 20, 15, etc.)
- Live chart preview below (regenerated on each data change)
- Insert/Cancel buttons

**Feasibility**: FULL — all calculations and rendering are pure JS + SVG. No Canvas or animation. Pie slice calculation uses standard trigonometry. The D3 approach (scales, transitions) is not necessary here; simple linear interpolation is sufficient for the static chart.

### Data Persistence

All three features insert static HTML/SVG. There is no post-insertion editability (user must delete and re-insert to change). This is acceptable for a basic implementation and matches Word's behavior for simple charts/diagrams.

### HTML Safety

All user-entered text (icon names in filter, SmartArt item text, chart labels/values) is passed through WC.escapeHtml before insertion, or inserted as textContent rather than innerHTML.

### CSS Classes for Post-Insert Styling

- `.inline-icon` — for icons
- `.smartart` — root div for SmartArt diagrams (data-layout attribute for reference)
- `.chart-svg` — root SVG for charts

These allow future CSS theming or JavaScript interactivity (e.g., click-to-edit).

### Testing Strategy

1. **Icons**: 
   - Open icons picker, verify grid renders all icons
   - Type filter (e.g., "check"), verify only matching icons appear
   - Click an icon, verify it inserts at cursor
   - Verify icon color inherits from text color (apply red color, insert icon, icon should be red)

2. **SmartArt**:
   - Click SmartArt, select each layout
   - Enter 3-5 items in the data editor
   - Verify live preview updates as user types
   - Click Insert, verify diagram appears in document
   - Verify all boxes and connectors render correctly

3. **Chart**:
   - Select each chart type (Column, Bar, Line, Pie)
   - Edit data grid (change values, add series, remove columns)
   - Verify live preview updates on each change
   - Click Insert, verify chart renders correctly
   - For pie: verify slices sum to 100%
   - For column/bar: verify axis labels and gridlines

4. **Post-Insert**:
   - Verify charts/diagrams survive copy-paste within the document
   - Verify export to PDF includes SVG (Electron's HTML→PDF should work)
   - Verify print includes charts/diagrams
specs.testPlan:
**Manual Testing**:
1. Icons: Open picker, search "check" → should filter to check icon only. Click it → inline SVG at cursor. Select the icon, apply red color → icon should be red.

2. SmartArt: Insert > SmartArt > Basic List → data editor opens. Enter "Buy Groceries", "Cook Dinner", "Eat" → live preview shows 3 stacked boxes. Click Insert → diagram appears in document. Click elsewhere to deselect.

3. Chart: Insert > Chart → Column chart dialog. Keep default data. Click Insert → column chart appears. Zoom out and verify proportions look sensible. Change one data value and re-insert a different chart → new chart should be visually distinct.

4. Copy/Paste: Select an inserted icon/diagram/chart, copy, paste below → should appear identical.

5. Keyboard Navigation: Open Icons picker, type "sh" → filters to "share", "shape", etc. Press down arrow → selects next icon. Press Enter → inserts and closes picker.

6. Export: Save as PDF, verify charts/diagrams render in the PDF (via Electron's PDF export, which uses Chromium).

**Edge Cases**:
- Empty series in chart (0 values) → bar height is 0 (no rendering error)
- Chart with 1 data point → chart still renders (no division by zero)
- SmartArt with empty text fields → shows blank boxes (no error)
- Very long text in SmartArt box → text wraps or truncates (CSS overflow: hidden or text-overflow: ellipsis)
- Negative numbers in chart → handled gracefully (chart y-axis adjusts to include min/max range, even if negative)

**Performance**:
- Icons picker with 200 icons: filter and grid re-render should complete in <100ms even on older hardware
- SmartArt preview: ~10ms to render (simple HTML)
- Chart preview with 15 data points (3 series × 5 categories): ~20ms to render SVG and insert into DOM

specs.feature: Header & Footer + Page Number (Insert tab feature group)
specs.wordBehavior: Word allows users to insert headers/footers (top/bottom margin regions edited separately with "Header"/"Footer" mode dimming the body) and auto-updating page number fields (displayed on current page position or top/bottom/margins, with format options). Headers/footers are stored separately per document section, rendered on every printed/PDF page via Chrome's printToPDF displayHeaderFooter/headerTemplate/footerTemplate, and edited in a special mode. Page numbers auto-update based on the pagination engine (page count calculated from scroll height / page height).
specs.uiNeeded: YES. Required: (1) Header/Footer dropdown menu handlers in commands.js linking to a header/footer edit mode; (2) Modal or taskpane (if lightweight) to enter header/footer editing mode with its own contenteditable region and exit button; (3) Page Number dropdown menu with position options (Top of Page, Bottom of Page, Page Margins, Current Position) and Format Page Numbers dialog; (4) Visual indicator that body is dimmed/read-only while in header/footer mode; (5) Toggle or button to remove headers/footers.
specs.implementationPlan:
ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW: 1. **Data Model** — Add to WC.Editor or a new WC.HeaderFooter module: - headerContent: string (HTML), footerContent: string (HTML) - pageNumberPosition: 'none' | 'top' | 'bottom' | 'margin' (per document) - pageNumberFormat: 'decimal' | 'roman' | 'alpha' | etc. (for future use, store in data attributes) - headerEditMode: boolean (true when user is editing header/footer; body is dimmed) 2. **UI Layout Changes** — editor.css + HTML structure: - Add hidden #header-region and #footer-region divs (styled as non-editable at first, revealed only during edit mode) - Style them: position absolute, top/bottom of #editor, full width, small height (e.g., 80px) - In edit mode: borders/background show they're separate; body (#editor main content) opacity 0.5 or lighter background - Page-break guides and page margins should be aware of header/footer height when calculating pagination 3. **Header/Footer Edit Mode** (new module: header-footer.js): - Function: enterHeaderFooterMode(type: 'header' | 'footer') - Create a modal or taskpane overlay with title "Editing Header" or "Editing Footer" - Show contenteditable div with current header/footer HTML - Provide: Close button (save + exit), Delete button (clear + exit), link to "Design > Options" (placeholder for Full-Document/First-Page-Different/etc.) - Disable main editor while active - On close/save: write contenteditable.innerHTML to WC.Editor.headerContent/footerContent, mark dirty, exit mode - Function: exitHeaderFooterMode() - Remove overlay, re-enable main editor, focus #editor 4. **Page Number Field Implementation**: - Create special elements - Insert via insertHTML in commands.js (pageNumber handler) - During on-screen render: Calculate current page dynamically using Editor.currentPage() and update the span's text - Listener: on repaginate(), recalculate all .page-number-field spans across the document - On PDF export: keep spans as-is; PDF will show actual page number (handled by printToPDF) 5. **Commands Handler** (in commands.js): - H.header = dropdown → flyout with: "Edit Header", "Remove Header", options for blank/predefined layouts - Edit: calls WC.HeaderFooter.enterHeaderFooterMode('header') - Remove: clears WC.Editor.headerContent, hides #header-region - H.footer = dropdown → flyout with: "Edit Footer", "Remove Footer", options for blank/predefined layouts - Edit: calls WC.HeaderFooter.enterHeaderFooterMode('footer') - Remove: clears WC.Editor.footerContent, hides #footer-region - H.pageNumber = dropdown → flyout with: "Top of Page", "Bottom of Page", "Page Margins", "Current Position", "Format Page Numbers", "Remove Page Numbers" - Top/Bottom/Margins: inserts placeholder; select position and insert page number field at top/bottom/margin region - Current Position: inserts page number field at cursor - Format Page Numbers: open dialog to pick format (decimal, roman, etc.) — dialog updates data-format on existing fields - Remove: deletes all .page-number-field spans 6. **Page Number Dialog** (WC.Dialogs.pageNumberFormat): - Radio buttons: "1, 2, 3", "i, ii, iii", "I, II, III", "a, b, c", "A, B, C" - Option to include chapter number (placeholder) - Start at: input field to set starting page number - On OK: update data-format on all .page-number-field spans; re-render 7. **Pagination Integration**: - Modify Editor.repaginate() to: - Calculate current page count (already done) - For each .page-number-field span in document, update textContent based on its position and currentPage() - New helper: pageNumberFormatted(pageNum, format) → returns "1", "i", "A", etc. based on format 8. **PDF Export Integration** (main.js printToPDF): - Pass headerTemplate and footerTemplate if header/footer content exists - headerTemplate: HTML
containing header content (must inline all CSS) - footerTemplate: HTML
containing footer content + auto page number if configured for top/bottom - For "Page Margins" position: render header/footer in #header-region/#footer-region on-screen; PDF handles via margins - For "Current Position" page numbers: pass displayHeaderFooter: true, let Chrome's printToPDF insert page numbers (or use headerTemplate/footerTemplate with macro) - Caveat: Electron's printToPDF does NOT support page-number macros in headerTemplate; work-around: if page numbers exist, inject them into headerTemplate/footerTemplate as static values (one template per page) OR use Chrome's built-in page numbering in margins 9. **Save/Load (files.js)**: - Store header/footer content in document HTML as hidden tags or data attributes on : - - - - - On file load (WC.Files.open): parse meta tags, restore WC.Editor.headerContent/footerContent - On getHTML: inject meta tags before returning HTML - On export DOCX (main.js writeDocx): pass header/footer in htmlToDocx options if supported; otherwise embed in body HTML with CSS page breaks 10. **CSS/Visual**: - #header-region, #footer-region: display: none by default; shown only when content exists or in edit mode - In header/footer edit mode: #editor { opacity: 0.4; pointer-events: none; } to dim body - Taskpane/modal for header/footer editing: fixed position, z-index 9500, with close/save buttons - Page number field: small border/background on-screen for visibility (e.g., border: 1px dashed #ccc; padding: 0 2px) - Remove border/style in print/PDF view (media print) REAL BEHAVIOR vs. INFEASIBLE PARTS: - REAL: Header/footer editing mode, separate regions, page number field insertion and pagination - REAL: On-screen rendering of headers/footers (if set to "Page Margins") via CSS positioning - REAL: PDF export via Electron's printToPDF with headerTemplate/footerTemplate injection - INFEASIBLE (HONEST PLACEHOLDERS): - Built-in header/footer gallery (Word's preset layouts) — placeholder: just "Edit Header" (user starts blank) - Full-Document vs. First-Page-Different vs. Odd/Even section management — placeholder: single header/footer for all pages - Linking headers/footers between sections — placeholder: document-level only - Page number field macro support in printToPDF (Chrome limitation) — work-around: inject page numbers into headerTemplate/footerTemplate at export time, or use margin-based positioning - "Save to Gallery" — not implemented; document-level storage only

specs.feature: Links: Bookmark + Cross-reference (Insert tab, Links group)
specs.wordBehavior: 1. BOOKMARK: Insert dialog allows user to (a) add a new named bookmark at the caret (stored as hidden anchor with name/id attributes), (b) list all existing bookmarks in the document, (c) navigate to or delete a bookmark. 2. CROSS-REFERENCE: Insert dialog allows user to select a target (heading, bookmark, page number) and insert a clickable hyperlink that jumps to that target; reference text is configurable ("see above/below", heading text, or page number).
specs.uiNeeded: Two modal dialogs: (1) "Insert Bookmark" with text input for bookmark name, list of existing bookmarks (with Delete and Go To buttons), and OK/Cancel. (2) "Insert Cross-Reference" with dropdown for reference type (Heading, Bookmark, Page Number), list of available targets, preview of reference text, and OK/Cancel. Both modal, no task panes required.
specs.implementationPlan: ## Data Model & Storage **Bookmarks:** - Stored as `` inserted at the caret (hidden via CSS). - ID and data-bookmark attributes both hold the sanitized bookmark name (lowercase, alphanumeric + underscores, max 40 chars). - CSS in editor.css: `.bookmark { display: none; }` (invisible, does not affect layout). - Query: `E().node.querySelectorAll('a.bookmark')` to enumerate all bookmarks; `a.id` or `a.dataset.bookmark` to get the name. **Headings:** - Already exist in the document as h1/h2/h3 elements (detected by navigation pane in dialogs.js, line 263). - No additional markup needed; use textContent as heading label, or fall back to "(empty heading)" if blank. ## Implementation: Handlers in commands.js **H.bookmark:** ```javascript H.bookmark = () => WC.Dialogs.insertBookmark(); ``` **H.crossReference:** ```javascript H.crossReference = () => WC.Dialogs.insertCrossReference(); ``` ## Implementation: Dialogs in dialogs.js ### D.insertBookmark() Opens a modal with: 1. Input field: "Bookmark name" (alphanumeric + underscore, auto-sanitize on input) 2. List of existing bookmarks with "Delete" button per item (left-side list) 3. "Go To" button to navigate to selected bookmark 4. "OK" button to insert at caret 5. "Cancel" button **Insert logic:** - Sanitize name: remove non-alphanumeric/underscore, lowercase, trim to 40 chars. If empty, toast "Name required" and stay open. - Check for duplicates: if name already exists, toast "Bookmark exists" and stay open (or auto-select in list). - Insert HTML: `E().insertHTML('');` - On OK, close dialog and toast "Bookmark 'NAME' inserted." **Navigation logic:** - On "Go To" click: find the bookmark element, call `element.scrollIntoView({ block: 'start', behavior: 'smooth' })`. **Delete logic:** - Remove the bookmark element from the DOM: `bookmarkElement.remove(); E().dirty = true; E().repaginate(); E().updateStatus();` ### D.insertCrossReference() Opens a modal with: 1. Dropdown: "Reference type" → [Heading, Bookmark, Page Number] 2. List of targets (populated from type): headings are h1/h2/h3 in document, bookmarks from querySelectorAll, page number is just the current page. 3. Preview span: shows what the inserted text will look like (e.g., "See Heading 1: Example Heading" or "page 3") 4. "OK" button to insert 5. "Cancel" button **Insert logic:** - Build a clickable anchor: `REFERENCE_TEXT ` - href is `#` + the bookmark ID or heading ID (if heading, generate or use existing id if present; if not, generate `heading-HASH` where HASH is first 8 chars of base64(heading text)). - class="cross-ref" allows styling (optional blue underline for visual distinction). - On click, JS can jump: attach a handler to all cross-refs to call `scrollIntoView()` on the target element. **Target enumeration:** - **Heading:** query h1/h2/h3, use textContent or "(empty heading)"; on insert, ensure heading has an id attribute (generate if missing). - **Bookmark:** query a.bookmark, use data-bookmark. - **Page Number:** show "Current page (X)" as single option; insert "page X" text with no href (static). **Reference text options (based on type):** - Heading: "Heading X: [text]" or just "[text]" - Bookmark: "[bookmark name]" or "See above/below" (infer from position) - Page Number: "page X" ## Integration Points 1. **commands.js:** - Add H.bookmark and H.crossReference handlers calling WC.Dialogs.insertBookmark/insertCrossReference. - Already in ribbon-data.js as cmd="bookmark" and cmd="crossReference". 2. **dialogs.js:** - Add D.insertBookmark and D.insertCrossReference functions. - Assign to WC.Dialogs.insertBookmark and WC.Dialogs.insertCrossReference. 3. **editor.js:** - No changes needed (insertHTML, selectedBlocks, etc. already support the feature). 4. **editor.css:** - Add: `.bookmark { display: none; }` to hide bookmarks visually. - Optional: `.cross-ref { color: #0563C1; text-decoration: underline; cursor: pointer; }` for styling. 5. **app.js or index.html script load order:** - No changes; dialogs.js is already loaded before commands.js (line 46-47 in index.html). ## Navigation (Click-to-Jump) Cross-reference anchors must jump on click: 1. In dialogs.js or a new module (or attach to Editor), after cross-refs are inserted: ```javascript E().node.addEventListener('click', (e) => { if (e.target.classList.contains('cross-ref') && e.target.href) { e.preventDefault(); const id = e.target.href.split('#')[1]; const el = id && E().node.querySelector('#' + id); if (el) el.scrollIntoView({ block: 'start', behavior: 'smooth' }); } }); ``` - Attach once in Editor.init(). 2. For bookmarks, cross-refs link to `#BOOKMARK_ID`, which a browser native anchor jump would handle if it were a real href. To be safe, we intercept click and use scrollIntoView for smooth behavior. ## Heading IDs If a heading lacks an id: 1. Generate one: `heading-HASH` where HASH = first 8 chars of base64(textContent). 2. Set `heading.id = generatedId` before inserting the cross-ref. 3. This ensures the heading is always reachable and survives document edits (as long as the heading element exists). ## Feasibility Notes - **Fully Feasible:** bookmark insertion, bookmark list, heading enumeration, basic cross-ref to heading/bookmark. - **Genuine Limitation (Placeholder):** "Update automatically if content moves" is beyond scope of this clone (would require a change tracker / undo/redo integration, which is not implemented). Cross-refs are static hyperlinks; if a heading is renamed or moved, the reference text does not update. This is honest for a local clone and matches Word's advanced feature set.
specs.testPlan: 1. **Bookmark Insertion & Management:** - Open Insert Bookmark dialog; type "intro" in name field; click OK. - Verify `` is inserted at the caret (invisible on screen). - Open dialog again; "intro" appears in the list. - Click "Delete" next to "intro"; verify element is removed from DOM and list refreshes. - Insert multiple bookmarks; verify list grows. 2. **Bookmark Navigation:** - Insert bookmarks at multiple locations. - Open Insert Bookmark dialog; select "intro" from list; click "Go To". - Verify page scrolls to the bookmark location. 3. **Cross-Reference Insertion (Heading):** - Create a heading: "## My Section" (h2 element). - Open Insert Cross-Reference; select type "Heading". - List shows "My Section"; click OK. - Verify link like `See Heading 2: My Section` is inserted. - Heading element now has id="heading-XXXX" if it didn't before. 4. **Cross-Reference Insertion (Bookmark):** - Insert bookmark "background" early in doc. - Later, open Insert Cross-Reference; select type "Bookmark". - List shows "background"; click OK. - Verify `background` is inserted. 5. **Cross-Reference Click Navigation:** - Insert a cross-ref to a bookmark or heading. - Click the cross-ref link. - Verify page scrolls to the target with smooth animation. 6. **Page Number Reference:** - Open Insert Cross-Reference; select type "Page Number". - Click OK. - Verify "page 1" (or current page number) is inserted as plain text (no hyperlink). 7. **Edge Cases:** - Insert bookmark with name " A/B #$% " → sanitizes to "ab". - Insert two bookmarks both named "test" → second insert toasts "Bookmark exists" and stays open. - Delete a heading that has a cross-ref to it → cross-ref link breaks (smooth graceful failure, no JS error). - Insert bookmark, then export PDF → bookmark should be invisible in PDF output (handled by CSS display:none).

specs.feature: Symbols and Equations Insert Tab Feature Group: Full Character Map Dialog + Equation Gallery with Building Blocks
specs.wordBehavior:
SYMBOLS (Full Dialog):
- User clicks Insert > Symbol > More Symbols (currently unimplemented)
- Opens a modal dialog with:
  * Scrollable character grid (~48 chars/row, organized by Unicode range)
  * Font selector (12+ installed fonts: Calibri, Arial, Times New Roman, Cambria, Georgia, Wingdings, Symbol, etc.)
  * Subset selector (Latin, Greek, Currency, Mathematical Operators, Arrows, Geometric Shapes, Miscellaneous Symbols, etc.)
  * Recently-used symbol history (last 10 inserted)
  * Large preview of selected character with Unicode code point displayed
  * Insert button or double-click to insert; dialog stays open for multiple insertions (Word behavior)
  * Recently-used list updates after each insertion
- Currently: quick 8x8 grid flyout with 40 hardcoded symbols + "More Symbols…" stub

EQUATIONS (Gallery + Structured Input):
- User clicks Insert > Equation
- Opens a modal dialog with:
  * Equation gallery: ~15 common equations (quadratic formula, Pythagorean, E=mc², integrals, summation, limits, etc.)
  * Click a preset equation → it populates the input textarea
  * Manual text editor with placeholder "Type equation (Unicode/ASCII math)"
  * Preview span showing the equation as it will render
  * Insert button closes dialog; recently-used equations could be tracked (honest placeholder: track in memory only, not persistent)
- Currently: textarea + 6 hardcoded presets as button chips
